answers.Q1_art: I'm a tattoo artist specializing in neo-traditional and Japanese-inspired work. I've been at this for about six years now. I work out of a shop in Austin but also do private appointments. My Instagram is how most people find me.
answers.Q2_background: Started drawing when I was like 14, got obsessed with tattoo culture in high school. Did an apprenticeship right after college instead of using my degree. Best decision I ever made.
answers.Q3_best_year: Finally getting my own station at the shop. After two years of working under someone else, having my own space and my own clients felt like I made it.
answers.Q4_theft: Yeah, about three years ago I found a shop in Dallas had one of my custom designs in their flash sheet. A client sent me their Instagram post. It was literally my design, line for line.
answers.Q5_feeling: I felt sick. Like physically sick. This was a piece I spent 15 hours designing for a client. Someone just took it and started selling it. I couldn't sleep that night.
answers.Q6_worries: That someone will steal my designs before I even get to tattoo them. I show clients work in progress on Instagram and I know other artists screenshot everything.
answers.Q7_magic_wand: A way to automatically find everywhere my work is posted online and prove it's mine. Like a digital fingerprint for my art.
answers.Q8_current_protection: Honestly? Not much. I put watermarks on my Instagram posts. I registered a few designs with the copyright office but that's expensive and time-consuming.
answers.Q9_time_money: I've spent maybe $200 on copyright registrations. Time-wise? Probably 20 hours a year dealing with takedown requests and worrying about it.
answers.Q10_worked_not: Watermarks work kinda but people can crop them out. Copyright registration works but it's slow and costs money for each piece.
answers.Q11_free_tool_reaction: A free tool that does all that? I'd be skeptical honestly. If it's free, how do they make money? But I'd try it if other artists vouch for it.
answers.Q12_trust_suspicious: If it asked for my Instagram login that's suspicious. If it just asks for my images and does the scanning, that feels safer. I'd trust it more if I could see reviews from other artists.
answers.Q13_stolen_art_action: Send me an alert with the screenshot and the URL. Let me decide what to do. Don't auto-send takedown notices because sometimes I want to reach out directly.
answers.Q14_friend_recommendation: I'd say 'Bro, this thing found three of my designs being used without permission. You need to check it out.' I'd show them the proof.
answers.Q16_move_number_up: If I could see it actually work on my existing posts first. Like a free scan of my Instagram to see what it finds. That would prove it.
answers.Q17_anything_else: Just make sure it's easy to use. I'm not a tech person. If I have to watch a 10-minute tutorial I'm out.

answers.Q1_art: I own a small tattoo studio in Portland. We specialize in botanical and fine line work. I've been tattooing for ten years and teaching workshops on the side.
answers.Q2_background: Art school dropout who found tattooing. Started as an apprentice at 24, opened my own shop at 30. It's been a wild ride.
answers.Q3_best_year: Opening my own studio. Going from employee to employer was terrifying but it's the best thing I've done professionally.
answers.Q4_theft: Constantly. My flash designs get stolen by shops in other cities. Last month I found my entire flash sheet on a shop's Instagram in Texas.
answers.Q5_feeling: At this point I'm angry more than sad. It's like they're stealing from my family. These designs feed my kid.
answers.Q6_worries: That I'll stop being creative because I'm too busy protecting my work. I've started holding back on posting new designs.
answers.Q7_magic_wand: Something that makes theft impossible. Or at least makes it so obvious that the original artist gets credit immediately.
answers.Q8_current_protection: I use a service that monitors social media for my images. Costs me $50 a month. Also register copyrights quarterly.
answers.Q9_time_money: About $600 a year on monitoring and copyright. Time-wise? 40+ hours dealing with takedowns and legal stuff.
answers.Q10_worked_not: The monitoring service catches about 60% of theft. Copyright registration is ironclad but slow.
answers.Q11_free_tool_reaction: If it's truly free and works, I'd be suspicious about how they monetize. But I'd try it as a secondary tool alongside my paid service.
answers.Q12_trust_suspicious: If it's from a known company or endorsed by tattoo associations I'd trust it more. If it's some random startup I've never heard of, I'd be cautious.
answers.Q13_stolen_art_action: I want options. Auto-takedown for obvious thefts, manual review for borderline cases. And I want to see the evidence before acting.
answers.Q14_friend_recommendation: I'd bring it up at our next shop meeting. 'Hey guys, this tool found my stolen designs. You should check your stuff too.'
answers.Q16_move_number_up: If it could integrate with my existing workflow. If it works alongside my current monitoring service instead of replacing it.
answers.Q17_anything_else: Make it scalable. I have 200+ designs to protect. If it can handle that volume I'm interested.

answers.Q1_art: I'm a flash artist and tattoo artist. I focus on traditional American style flash sheets. I sell prints and do tattoo work out of a shop in Nashville.
answers.Q2_background: Started drawing flash in college, got an apprenticeship after graduation. Been doing it three years.
answers.Q3_best_year: Having my first flash sheet go viral on TikTok. Got me tons of clients and print sales.
answers.Q4_theft: Yeah, a shop in Atlanta posted one of my flash sheets as their own work. Their clients were getting tattoos from my designs.
answers.Q5_feeling: It hurt because that sheet took me weeks. And they were making money off it while I was struggling to pay rent.
answers.Q6_worries: That I'll never be able to make a real living because people keep stealing my work. Social media makes it too easy.
answers.Q7_magic_wand: A way to make my designs un-stealable. Like they'd be tied to me digitally somehow.
answers.Q8_current_protection: Nothing really. I put my name on my flash sheets but people just crop it out.
answers.Q9_time_money: Zero dollars. Maybe 5 hours a year sending angry DMs to shops that steal my stuff.
answers.Q10_worked_not: Nothing has worked. DMs get ignored. Instagram reports go nowhere.
answers.Q11_free_tool_reaction: Free? I'd try it immediately. I can't afford paid tools right now.
answers.Q12_trust_suspicious: If it promised too much I'd be suspicious. Like if it said it would get me money for stolen designs, that's too good to be true.
answers.Q13_stolen_art_action: Just tell me where it's being used. I'll handle the rest.
answers.Q14_friend_recommendation: I'd post about it on TikTok if it worked. 'This free thing found my stolen art, check it out.'
answers.Q16_move_number_up: If I could see it work on my TikTok content first. That's where most of my theft happens.
answers.Q17_anything_else: Make it work with TikTok specifically. That's where artists like me live.

answers.Q1_art: I do Chicano-style tattoos and custom portraits. Been tattooing for eight years in Miami. My work is very detailed and personal to each client.
answers.Q2_background: Grew up in the tattoo culture here in Miami. My uncle was a legend in the scene. Started apprenticing with him at 21.
answers.Q3_best_year: Getting featured in Tattoo Magazine. That changed everything for my career.
answers.Q4_theft: Yes, a shop in California used one of my portrait designs without permission. They claimed they 'inspired' it but it was identical.
answers.Q5_feeling: Disrespected. Like they don't understand the work that goes into a custom portrait. It's not just drawing, it's capturing someone's essence.
answers.Q6_worries: That my reputation will suffer because people will think I copied someone else's version of my own work.
answers.Q7_magic_wand: A way to timestamp my work so there's no question who created it first.
answers.Q8_current_protection: I keep detailed records of my process. Screenshots of WIPs, client conversations, timestamps.
answers.Q9_time_money: About $100 a year on cloud storage for my records. 15 hours organizing and documenting everything.
answers.Q10_worked_not: My documentation has helped in a few disputes but it's a lot of manual work.
answers.Q11_free_tool_reaction: I'd be interested if it could automate my documentation process. That would save me a lot of time.
answers.Q12_trust_suspicious: If it required access to my client information I'd be very suspicious. My clients trust me with their personal stories.
answers.Q13_stolen_art_action: I want to know immediately. And I want proof that I can use in a legal dispute if needed.
answers.Q14_friend_recommendation: I'd recommend it to artists who do custom work like me. The ones who put their heart into every piece.
answers.Q16_move_number_up: If it could prove it protects my process documentation too. Not just the final images.
answers.Q17_anything_else: Please understand that tattoo art is personal. We're not just selling images, we're creating meaning.

answers.Q1_art: I'm a geometric and dotwork tattoo artist. Been doing it for four years. I post all my work on Instagram and TikTok.
answers.Q2_background: Was a graphic designer before switching to tattooing. The transition was rough but worth it.
answers.Q3_best_year: Building a loyal client base. I now have a three-month waitlist.
answers.Q4_theft: Not that I know of. But I worry about it constantly.
answers.Q5_feeling: I'd feel betrayed. These designs are my babies.
answers.Q6_worries: That the moment I start getting successful, someone will steal my designs and undercut me.
answers.Q7_magic_wand: Something that prevents theft before it happens. Like a digital lock on my images.
answers.Q8_current_protection: I use Instagram's built-in features and keep my account private sometimes.
answers.Q9_time_money: Zero. I haven't had to deal with theft yet.
answers.Q10_worked_not: Haven't tested anything because I haven't had the problem yet.
answers.Q11_free_tool_reaction: I'd sign up right away. Prevention is better than dealing with theft after the fact.
answers.Q12_trust_suspicious: If it seemed too complicated I'd skip it. I need something simple that just works.
answers.Q13_stolen_art_action: Alert me and tell me how to prevent it from spreading.
answers.Q14_friend_recommendation: I'd mention it in our shop group chat. 'Hey, this thing might be useful.'
answers.Q16_move_number_up: If it could show me what it's already found on my existing content. Proof of concept.
answers.Q17_anything_else: I'm proactive about this stuff. I'd rather protect myself now than regret it later.

answers.Q13_stolen_art_action: Automated takedowns for clear cases. Manual alerts for ones that need review.

answers.Q17_anything_else: Make sure it works internationally. My art gets stolen globally.

answers.Q14_friend_recommendation: I'd tell other shop owners at industry events. 'This tool might help with the theft problem.'
answers.Q16_move_number_up: If it could show me a report of all my protected work and where it's being used.
answers.Q17_anything_else: The industry needs this. Theft is an epidemic and we need solutions.

answers.Q1_art: I own a tattoo studio in Houston. We specialize in South Asian-inspired designs and custom work. Been tattooing for 11 years.
answers.Q2_background: Family expected me to be a doctor. I chose art. It was a difficult conversation but they're proud now.
answers.Q3_best_year: When my work got featured in a major tattoo publication. That put my shop on the map.

answers.Q6_worries: That sacred cultural designs will be disrespected and commodified.
